title: What it actually looks like: hard on yourself, gentle with people
caption:
Most men flip it.

They're hard on the people around them — sharp word, kept score, eye-roll at someone else's failure. And soft on their own sin — calling it a weakness, agreeing to accountability and never actually giving it.

If you'd call a friend out for his laziness, call it out in the mirror first. The standard doesn't lower when it's you.

Every unit of energy you spend scrutinizing someone else's obedience is borrowed from your own formation. You can't run both. Pick one.

Jesus wept over the city. He drove out the men profiting from the temple. Both were right. Tenderness for people in pain. Fierceness for sin that harms. A man of faith learns the difference.

Fierceness for righteousness. Tenderness for people. Get them in the right places.

Save this and share it with the man who needs to hear it.

#faith #christianmen #godlymen #manofgod #realtalk #discipline #christianity #maturity
theme: He's hard on himself, gentle with others.
